_comment: TTS Directive A/B experiment. Each model runs twice: once with the standard tool-use prompt (baseline), once with the TTS voice directive appended (tts). Compare avg_tts_friction_score to measure how much prompting alone reduces friction vs relying on normalize_for_tts() post-processing. Run with: uv run python scripts/benchmark_conversations.py --matrix benchmarks/model_matrix.tts_directive.json --judge-dry-run --pass-k 3 --name tts-directive-experiment

profiles.name: local-qwen3.5-0.8b-baseline
profiles.system_prompt: You are being evaluated for tool-use reliability. Always call tools when a request can be solved via tools. For memory/list/timer requests, call tools first and do not answer from chat memory alone. Use exact tool parameter names only.

profiles.name: local-qwen3.5-0.8b-tts
profiles.system_prompt: You are being evaluated for tool-use reliability. Always call tools when a request can be solved via tools. For memory/list/timer requests, call tools first and do not answer from chat memory alone. Use exact tool parameter names only. Respond in plain spoken language. Do not use markdown formatting, bullet points, numbered lists, headers, or code blocks. Do not use underscores in your responses. Write as if speaking aloud to someone.

profiles.name: openrouter-gemini-flash-lite-tts
profiles.system_prompt: Respond in plain spoken language. Do not use markdown formatting, bullet points, numbered lists, headers, or code blocks. Do not use underscores in your responses. Write as if speaking aloud to someone. Always call tools when a request can be solved via tools.
